danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures and minions on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; player must avoid or neutralize
Heightened danger; significant challenge ahead requiring skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Freezing warehouse, collapsing ceiling, amusement park obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Structural or environmental threat to player safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Character knockback and visual feedback on contact
danger_signals.meaning: Player health loss; three hits maximum before life loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Acorns, crates, barrels, balls scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resources for combat and health recovery; interactive world elements

safety_signals.visual_form: Chip or Dale visible on screen (2-player mode)
safety_signals.meaning: Player can throw/use partner as weapon; mutual protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Linear level design with clear forward path; amusement park hub structure
safety_signals.meaning: Controllable progression; player agency in level order (amusement park stages 1-3)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chip and Dale (chipmunk characters); team identity
Fat Cat as leader; minions follow his orders
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied minion types (rabbit bomb-setter, warehouse thugs, ghost-like entities)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Prison (Fat Cat breaks out), warehouse, docked cargo ship, amusement park control room

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Urn of the Pharaoh as containment vessel
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghost entities released from urn; distinct supernatural enemy class
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Warehouse fills with supernatural presence; mystical artifact origin

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Restaurant setting with danger elements; confined urban space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player pursues enemy rabbit; time-sensitive rescue scenario

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Prison escape - Fat Cat breaks free
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Docks and cargo ship environment; institutional setting transitions to maritime
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rangers chase Fat Cat; escalating threat level

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Urn of Pharaoh released - supernatural crisis
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Warehouse fills with ghostly entities; visual shift to supernatural/horror aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy composition changes to ghost-type entities; urgency increases

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation - Fat Cat's robot challenge
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Amusement park attractions (Clock Tower, Western World, Future World); control room with giant mechanical boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle arena; Fat Cat pilots giant robot modeled after himself; ceiling collapse environmental threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collapsing environment; inability to locate Fat Cat visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced retreat; Fat Cat escapes but Rangers vow to stop him next time

environmental_storytelling.example: Restaurant → Docks → Ship → Warehouse → Amusement Park traces criminal conspiracy escalation and artifact threat
Freezing refrigerator traps communicate danger escalation; warehouse ghost-fill conveys supernatural plot twist; collapsing ceiling creates time pressure
Amusement park final stage uses three themed worlds (Clock Tower/time, Western World/frontier, Future World/technology) reflecting game's breadth before control room confrontation
Fat Cat's appearance and movement patterns signal plot progression; minion types reflect current threat (organized crime vs. supernatural)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Orders subordinates (rabbit bomb-setter); breaks prison; flees from Rangers; pilots giant robot; escapes at final confrontation
Pursue threats; pick up and throw objects; can throw each other as weapon (2-player mechanic); navigate hazards; escape forced traps
Stationary or patrol patterns; throw-able objects or combat threats; varied AI based on location (warehouse thugs, ghost entities)
Swarm warehouse; distinct enemy behavior patterns; higher threat level than mundane minions
Large-scale attacks; requires projectile/object interaction to defeat; occupies control room arena

notes: Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers 2 uses Disney cartoon source material to establish faction identity (heroic chipmunk team vs. criminal Fat Cat organization). World-state communication relies on environmental transitions and enemy type shifts rather than explicit UI indicators. The game communicates danger through: (1) enemy presence and type variation, (2) environmental hazards tied to plot (freezer trap, ghost-filled warehouse, collapsing ceiling), (3) boss scale indicating narrative climax. Cooperative mechanics (player-throw-player) reinforce team faction identity. Linear stage progression with branching amusement park segment provides player agency. Narrative tension escalates through location changes and threat mutation (crime → supernatural → mechanical).
